CREATE TABLE test_strings (input_string VARCHAR, trim_characters VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES ('foo bar baz', 'z');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES ('no characters to trim', 'x');
SELECT rtrim(input_string, trim_characters) AS trimmed_string FROM test_strings;
SELECT rtrim(rtrim(input_string, trim_characters), trim_characters) AS double_trim FROM test_strings;
CREATE TABLE other_strings AS SELECT rtrim(input_string, trim_characters) FROM test_strings WHERE input_string LIKE '>>>>%';
SELECT trimmed_string, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT rtrim(input_string, trim_characters) AS trimmed_string FROM test_strings) AS subquery GROUP BY trimmed_string;
CREATE TABLE complex_trim AS SELECT rtrim(input_string || 'additional', trim_characters) FROM test_strings WHERE LENGTH(input_string) > 5;
SELECT a.input_string, b.trimmed_string FROM test_strings a JOIN (SELECT rtrim(input_string, trim_characters) AS trimmed_string FROM test_strings) b ON a.input_string = b.trimmed_string;
